raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need command to print last word in file. Use awk or rev. For last word: awk '{for(i=1;i<=NF;i++) last=$i} END{print last}'. Or simpler: awk '{print $NF}'? That prints last field of last line. But last word overall? If file has multiple lines, last word overall is last field of last line. So awk '{print $NF}' works. But if want last word of entire file ignoring newlines? Probably last word of file. So: awk '{print $NF}' /workspace/dir1/long.txt | tail -n 1? Actually awk prints last field of each line. We want last word of file: use tail -n 1 | awk '{print $NF}'. So command: tail -n 1 /workspace/dir1/long.txt | awk '{print $NF}'. Provide that.
